explanationZhTw: 「食堂；餐廳」, 常指學校、工廠或機關裡供餐的地方。
generatedExample: We ate in the office canteen every weekday.

explanationZhTw: 「排隊的人龍；等待中的隊伍」。
generatedExample: The lunch queue was longer than usual today.

explanationZhTw: 「金屬格柵；柵欄式開口」，常見於窗戶、通風口或櫃台。
generatedExample: The cashier passed the receipt through the grille.

explanationZhTw: 「大量湧出；源源不絕地流出」。這裡形容蒸氣很快、很多地冒出來。
generatedExample: Warm air came pouring forth when I opened the oven.

explanationZhTw: 「刺鼻氣體、煙霧或氣味」；這裡偏向濃烈難聞的酒氣。
generatedExample: The fumes from the paint were hard to ignore.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，指「不起眼的小店、狹小簡陋的地方」。
generatedExample: They discovered a great noodle shop that was just a hole in the wall.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「nip」不是一般動詞，而是指「一小杯烈酒」；屬於酒吧用語。
generatedExample: He asked for a nip of whisky after dinner.

explanationZhTw: 「looking for」是常見片語動詞，意思是「在找、尋找」。這裡表示說話者剛好在找溫斯頓。
generatedExample: I was looking for you at the library this morning.

explanationZhTw: 轉身；把身體或方向轉向另一邊。
generatedExample: She turned round when she heard her name.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「朋友」不是字面上真正親近的朋友，而是帶有保留、甚至反諷的用法。
generatedExample: They were not really friends, just classmates who talked sometimes.

explanationZhTw: 同志；在這裡是政治化的稱呼，用來取代一般的「朋友」。
generatedExample: The leader told the soldiers to stay loyal to their comrades.

explanationZhTw: 語文學家；研究語言、文字、詞源等的人。
generatedExample: My professor is a philologist who studies ancient texts.

explanationZhTw: 專家；在某個領域有專門知識的人。
generatedExample: She is a specialist in child psychology.

explanationZhTw: 凸出的；這裡形容眼睛向外突出，帶有強烈的視覺印象。
generatedExample: The bird had protuberant eyes that made it look alert all the time.

explanationZhTw: `ask` 是常見動詞，意思是「詢問、請求」。這裡是單純的「問」。
generatedExample: I want to ask you a question.

explanationZhTw: `you'd got` 在英式英文裡常表示「你有／你擁有」，不是「你已經取得」。這裡是口語化的「have got」。
generatedExample: Have you got any time this afternoon?

explanationZhTw: `razor blades` 是「刮鬍刀片」。這是具體名詞片語，用來指剃鬚用的刀片。
generatedExample: I need to buy new razor blades.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「有罪的匆忙」，而是指說話時帶著一點心虛、歉意，且很急地回答。
generatedExample: She answered with a sort of guilty haste, as if she knew she was interrupting.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「到處、四處」；這裡表示他已經各個地方都找過了。
generatedExample: I looked all over the place for my keys.

explanationZhTw: 在否定句裡，any longer 表示「不再、再也不」；常用來說某事已經停止。
generatedExample: We don't live there any longer.

explanationZhTw: `hoard up` 表示大量囤積、存放起來不拿出來用；這裡暗示他把少數未用的刀片偷偷留著。
generatedExample: She was hoarding up canned food before the storm arrived.

explanationZhTw: 這裡 `famine` 不是指真正的饑荒，而是比喻「極度短缺、長期缺貨」。
generatedExample: There was a famine of clean drinking water after the earthquake.

explanationZhTw: `get hold of` 是片語動詞，意思是「取得、弄到手」。
generatedExample: I finally got hold of a spare charger for my phone.

explanationZhTw: `scrounge` 在這裡表示「四處湊、靠不太正當或不光明的方式弄到」。
generatedExample: He spent the afternoon scrounging for spare parts in old boxes.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `blade` 指「刀片；刮鬍刀片」，不是一般只想到的「刀刃」。
generatedExample: He replaced the blade before shaving this morning.

explanationZhTw: queue 指「排隊的人龍」；這裡是在描寫隊伍往前挪動。
generatedExample: We waited in the queue for twenty minutes.

explanationZhTw: jerk forward 指「突然向前一抽、猛地往前移動」；這裡強調隊伍不平順地前進。
generatedExample: The bus jerked forward when the driver changed gear.

explanationZhTw: halted 是「停下、停止前進」；比單純的 stop 更正式一點。
generatedExample: The march halted at the river.

explanationZhTw: greasy 指「油膩的、沾滿油的」；帶有髒污或不好摸的感覺。
generatedExample: My hands felt greasy after I ate the fries.

explanationZhTw: tray 是「托盤」；用來端食物或放小物件的平盤。
generatedExample: She carried the cups on a silver tray.

explanationZhTw: counter 這裡是「櫃檯、吧台」；不是「反擊」或「相反」的意思。
generatedExample: Please pay at the counter before you leave.

explanationZhTw: 指「囚犯、犯人」；在這裡是一般名詞，屬於基礎但值得注意的常用詞。
generatedExample: The prisoners were moved to another wing of the jail.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是單純「掛起來」，而是指「被絞死、被處以絞刑」；這是依上下文決定的特殊用法。
generatedExample: The criminal was hanged for his crimes.

explanationZhTw: 「indifferently」在這裡不是指「差不多」，而是指「冷淡地、漫不經心地」。用來表現溫斯頓說話時沒有熱情或興趣。
generatedExample: She answered indifferently and kept looking at her phone.

explanationZhTw: 「the flicks」是較舊的口語，意思是「電影／戲院」。在這裡是帶有時代感的說法，不是一般最常見的 movie。
generatedExample: We went to the flicks after dinner.

explanationZhTw: 不夠適當的；不足以達到需要的標準。這裡表示這個替代品遠遠不夠好。
generatedExample: The battery is inadequate for a full day of travel.

explanationZhTw: 替代品；代用品。這裡指拿來代替原本事物的人或東西。
generatedExample: We used a cheap substitute when the main ingredient was unavailable.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指目光在某人臉上慢慢掃過、游移不定，不是一般「漫遊」的意思。
generatedExample: Her eyes roved over the crowd before she found her friend.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「看穿某人、識破某人的企圖或偽裝」。
generatedExample: You can hide the facts, but I see through you.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純宗教上的「正統」，而是指立場、思想非常符合官方或主流標準。
generatedExample: Her views were orthodox, so she rarely challenged the system.

explanationZhTw: 表示因別人的痛苦或失敗而暗自得意、幸災樂禍的滿足感。
generatedExample: He described the loss with gloating satisfaction.

explanationZhTw: 指專門細節、規則或技術上的瑣碎問題，通常比較難懂。
generatedExample: The contract was full of technicalities that slowed the deal down.

explanationZhTw: 表示某人對某主題很有權威、很懂，說話給人可靠且有把握的感覺。
generatedExample: She gave an authoritative answer that settled the argument.

explanationZhTw: 指仔細檢視、嚴密審視，通常帶有被盯著看或被檢查的感覺。
generatedExample: The proposal faced close scrutiny from the committee.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `hanging` 是指「絞刑、吊死」；不是一般的「吊掛」意思，而是處決方式。
generatedExample: The newspaper reported the hanging at dawn.

explanationZhTw: `spoils it` 是「把事情弄砸、破壞效果」；這裡是說把腳綁起來會讓場面失去他想看的效果。
generatedExample: Too much explanation spoils it for the audience.

explanationZhTw: `above all` 表示「最重要的是、尤其是」；用來強調最在意的重點。
generatedExample: Above all, make sure the room is quiet.

explanationZhTw: `sticking right out` 指「整個伸出來、突出來」；`right` 在這裡是加強語氣，不是「右邊」。
generatedExample: Her hair was sticking right out after the wind.

explanationZhTw: `appeals to me` 是「對我有吸引力、合我胃口」；這裡表示他特別喜歡這個細節。
generatedExample: That style really appeals to me.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Nex` 是口語化、帶省略感的說法，意思接近「下一位／下一個」；用在喊人或點名時，表示輪到誰了。
generatedExample: "Next, please," said the cashier to the person at the front of the line.

explanationZhTw: `prole` 是 `proletarian` 的縮寫，指「無產階級的人；勞工階層的人」，這裡是小說語境中的稱呼，帶有社會階級色彩。
generatedExample: The novel follows a young prole living in the industrial quarter.

explanationZhTw: `ladle` 是「長柄湯勺；舀勺」，通常用來舀湯、醬汁或其他液體。
generatedExample: She used a ladle to serve soup into each bowl.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `dumped` 不是單純「丟掉」，而是指把食物很快地倒上/發下來，帶有粗糙、機械化的感覺。
generatedExample: The waiter dumped the plates onto the table and hurried away.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `regulation` 不是「調節」的意思，而是指「按規定供應的、標準配給的」；整個片語是在說一份制式餐點。
generatedExample: We were given a regulation breakfast at the camp.

explanationZhTw: `pannikin` 是小金屬杯或小鍋子，這裡指裝湯或燉菜的金屬容器，屬於較罕見、帶點舊式的詞。
generatedExample: He drank the soup from a small pannikin by the fire.

explanationZhTw: `saccharine` 在這裡不是形容「矯情甜膩」，而是指「人工甜味劑」；整個片語就是一顆代糖錠。
generatedExample: She put one saccharine tablet into her tea.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡，`telescreen` 不是一般日常英文詞，而是《1984》裡的特定裝置名稱，指兼具螢幕與監視功能的終端機。
generatedExample: The guard stared at the telescreen as if it were listening to every word.

explanationZhTw: `pick up` 是片語動詞，這裡表示「順路買／拿一杯」。
generatedExample: We should pick up some water before we leave.

explanationZhTw: `gin` 指琴酒，一種常見的烈酒；這裡是可數名詞用法，表示一杯琴酒。
generatedExample: He ordered a gin and tonic at the bar.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡表示「穿過人群或障礙物，小心而靈活地前進」。
generatedExample: We threaded our way through the crowd to reach the platform.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「拿起、提起」的意思，不是「接手工作」。
generatedExample: She took up the cup and drank slowly.

explanationZhTw: 意思是「鼓起勇氣、穩住膽子再去做某事」。
generatedExample: He collected his nerve before knocking on the office door.

explanationZhTw: 表示「大口吞下、急急喝下」，常帶有勉強或匆忙的感覺。
generatedExample: After running, he gulped the cold water in one breath.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是泛指「東西」，而是帶有貶義地指那團難以下嚥的飲料或食物。
generatedExample: What is this stuff? It tastes terrible.

explanationZhTw: 指快速、含糊、聽起來很亂的說話聲。
generatedExample: I could only catch fragments of his gabble across the noisy room.

explanationZhTw: 指「喧鬧、嘈雜、混亂」的狀態。
generatedExample: The announcement caused an uproar in the hall.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `getting on` 是片語動詞，意思是「進展得如何、進行得怎麼樣」。Winston 不是在問字典「上去」哪裡，而是在問編寫字典的工作進度。
generatedExample: How is your project getting on after the deadline change?

explanationZhTw: 慢慢地；以緩慢的速度。
generatedExample: He slowly opened the door.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純表示『在...上』，而是指『正在處理／正在做』。
generatedExample: I'm on the budget report this morning.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞；這裡指文法中的形容詞這個詞類。
generatedExample: In 'a blue sky,' blue is an adjective.

explanationZhTw: 引人入勝的；非常有趣、讓人著迷。
generatedExample: The museum has a fascinating history.

explanationZhTw: 「brightened up」是片語動詞，這裡表示他突然變得更有精神、更高興，不是字面上的「變亮」。
generatedExample: He brightened up when he heard the good news.

explanationZhTw: 「pannikin」指小金屬杯或小碗，屬於較少見、帶點古風或文學色彩的詞。
generatedExample: The old soldier drank tea from a battered pannikin.

explanationZhTw: 「take up」這裡不是「開始從事」，而是指把東西拿起來。
generatedExample: She took up the notebook and began to read.

explanationZhTw: 「hunk」在這裡表示一大塊食物，屬於口語用法；整個片語是「一大塊麵包」。
generatedExample: He ate a hunk of bread with the soup.

explanationZhTw: 「定本／最終定稿版」；在這裡指被視為最權威、最終確定的版本，不只是一般的「版本」。
generatedExample: This is the definitive edition of the handbook, so we should use it for all references.

explanationZhTw: 「把語言塑造成最後定型的樣子」；這裡的 shape 不是外形，而是指語言被改造成固定、封閉的樣貌。
generatedExample: The editors are getting the report into its final shape before the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 「再從頭學一次」；all over again 表示整個過程重新來過，強調先前學過的東西會被迫重學。
generatedExample: I forgot most of the vocabulary, so I had to learn it all over again.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「完全不是這樣／一點也不」；用來強烈否定前面剛說的想法。
generatedExample: He looked tired? Not a bit of it. He was actually very excited.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，字面是「把語言砍到見骨」，實際意思是大幅刪減到極度精簡、幾乎只剩核心部分。
generatedExample: The budget cuts have cut the team down to the bone.

explanationZhTw: 「過時的；淘汰的」；這裡指某些字詞在未來還沒到 2050 年之前就會失去使用價值。
generatedExample: Some technologies become obsolete very quickly.

explanationZhTw: adv. 「hungrily」在這裡不是字面上只指「很餓」，而是表示吃得很急、很用力，帶出人物當下的狀態。
generatedExample: She ate hungrily after skipping lunch all day.

explanationZhTw: 片語。意思是「吞下了幾口」，描述他只簡單吃了幾口就繼續說話。
generatedExample: He swallowed a couple of mouthfuls of soup before rushing out.

explanationZhTw: 一般用法；在這裡強調他邊吃邊繼續講話，顯示他很投入。
generatedExample: She continued speaking even when the room became noisy.

explanationZhTw: 片語。這裡表示「某種／有點像」，說話者在描述時帶有保留或不精確。
generatedExample: It was a sort of apology, but not a very sincere one.

explanationZhTw: 片語。指「學究式的熱情」或「過分在意細節的熱情」，帶有一點諷刺意味。
generatedExample: He explained the rules with a pedant's passion.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞。這裡不是「動畫的」，而是指「變得生動、有神采、表情活起來」。
generatedExample: Her face became animated when she talked about music.

explanationZhTw: 片語。指「帶嘲弄意味的表情」，不是單純的笑，而是有輕蔑或諷刺感。
generatedExample: He gave me a mocking expression when I missed the answer.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞。這裡表示「恍惚的、若有所思的、近乎出神的」，不是單指「愛作夢的」。
generatedExample: She had a dreamy look after hearing the good news.

explanationZhTw: `get rid of` 是片語動詞，意思是「去除、丟掉、淘汰」。這裡指把許多名詞刪減不用。
generatedExample: We should get rid of the duplicate records before importing the data.

explanationZhTw: `synonyms` 指「同義詞」。文中是在說不只同義詞，連反義詞也會被簡化。
generatedExample: The worksheet asks students to find synonyms for each word.

explanationZhTw: `antonyms` 指「反義詞」。這裡和同義詞並列，表示相反意思的字也算在內。
generatedExample: Cold and hot are antonyms.

explanationZhTw: `after all` 是語氣片語，意思接近「畢竟、說到底」，用來補充或強調前面的論點。
generatedExample: After all, we only have a limited budget.

explanationZhTw: `vague` 在這裡帶有「不夠精確、含糊籠統」的語感，不只是一般的「模糊」。
generatedExample: Her instructions were so vague that nobody knew what to do.

explanationZhTw: `In the end` 是常見慣用語，意思是「最後、終究」。這裡用來總結推論。
generatedExample: In the end, they chose the simpler option.

explanationZhTw: `afterthought` 指「事後才補上的想法或一句話」。這裡表示他後來才順口補充這件事。
generatedExample: She mentioned the warning as an afterthought at the end of the call.

explanationZhTw: 形容詞，表示「空洞的、乏味的、缺乏真誠或思想的」。這裡用來形容 Winston 的興奮感是表面的，不是真的投入。
generatedExample: Her praise sounded vapid, as if she were just repeating a line.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，表示「急切、殷切、渴望做某事的心情」。在這裡是說他臉上閃過一種急切的神情。
generatedExample: The children waited with eagerness for the concert to start.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是動作描寫，意思是「短暫地閃現、掠過」。不是字面上的飛過，而是說表情很快出現在臉上又消失。
generatedExample: A worried look flitted across his face before he answered.

explanationZhTw: 動詞，這裡不是「用儀器偵測」，而是「察覺、看出」。Syme 很快就看出對方其實不夠熱衷。
generatedExample: She detected a hint of sarcasm in his reply.

explanationZhTw: 片語，意思是「缺乏熱情、興致不高」。這裡強調 Winston 表面上有反應，但內在並不真的投入。
generatedExample: The manager noticed a lack of enthusiasm during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「欣賞」，而是指「真正理解並看出價值」；意思是溫斯頓還不夠懂新語。
generatedExample: She has a real appreciation of how difficult this job is.

explanationZhTw: `Newspeak` 是本段中的自造詞，指一種被刻意設計、壓縮詞彙的官方語言，不是一般英文裡的普通名詞。
generatedExample: In the novel, Newspeak is designed to limit how people can think.

explanationZhTw: 這是片語動詞，意思是「堅持使用、遵守」；這裡是指他其實更想繼續用舊語。
generatedExample: Please stick to the topic during the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `Oldspeak` 是本段的自造詞，指「舊語」或原本的正常語言，和 `Newspeak` 相對。
generatedExample: Some people still prefer Oldspeak because it can express subtle ideas.

explanationZhTw: `vagueness` 指「含糊、不清楚」；此處是在批評舊語的表達不夠精確。
generatedExample: The vagueness of the rule made it hard to follow.

explanationZhTw: 這個片語指「細微的語意差別」；這裡是說舊語保留很多微妙不同的意思。
generatedExample: The two words have different shades of meaning.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `grasp` 不是「抓住」，而是「理解、領會」；意思是他不懂刪減詞彙的美感。
generatedExample: I didn't grasp the point of the joke at first.

explanationZhTw: `vocabulary` 是「詞彙量、字彙」；這裡特別強調新語的詞彙是被設計成越來越少。
generatedExample: My vocabulary improved after I started reading every day.

explanationZhTw: 語境用法，表示他不敢放心讓自己開口，怕一說話就失去控制、說出不該說的話，或暴露情緒。
generatedExample: She stayed silent, not trusting herself to speak without crying.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，指「咬下一小塊；咬斷」。
generatedExample: She bit off a small piece of the cookie and smiled.

explanationZhTw: 名詞，指「碎片；小塊」；這裡是指麵包的一小塊。
generatedExample: He picked up a fragment of glass from the floor.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，指「繼續下去；接著說／做」。
generatedExample: After a short pause, he went on with his explanation.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指《1984》中的「新語」，一種刻意設計來縮小可表達和可思考範圍的官方語言。
generatedExample: The government kept inventing Newspeak to make criticism harder to express.

explanationZhTw: 指「思想罪」，也就是連腦中的想法都可能被當成犯罪。
generatedExample: In that world, even private doubt could count as thoughtcrime.

explanationZhTw: 指次要、附帶的意思；這裡表示詞語除了唯一的官方定義之外，其餘延伸含義都被消除。
generatedExample: The editor removed the subsidiary meanings and kept only the main definition.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是真的用手擦掉，而是指「抹除、消滅」某些含義。
generatedExample: Her name was rubbed out of the record after the scandal.

explanationZhTw: 指意識、思考和感知能涵蓋的範圍；在這裡暗示人的心智被逐步縮窄。
generatedExample: A broad education can expand the range of consciousness.

explanationZhTw: 這是文中自造詞，指透過強迫自己接受官方說法來控制對現實的認知。
generatedExample: The regime demanded reality-control instead of independent thinking.

explanationZhTw: 帶有神祕、近乎宗教式的滿足感；這裡暗示說話者對這套語言體系很著迷。
generatedExample: He spoke with mystical satisfaction, as if he had uncovered a sacred truth.

explanationZhTw: 表示「你有沒有想到／意識到」；不是字面上的「發生在你身上」。
generatedExample: It suddenly occurred to me that I had left my phone on the train.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Except--` 不是一般「除非／除了」的完整用法，而是說話時突然卡住、想補充但又中斷的起頭，語氣接近「可是……／不過……」；用來表現猶豫或自我修正。
generatedExample: Except-- I mean, we should probably check the answer again.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，表示「話到嘴邊、差一點就要說出口」。
generatedExample: The answer was on the tip of my tongue, but I couldn’t remember it in time.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「及時打住、收回自己正要說的話」的意思，不是一般的「檢查自己」。
generatedExample: She started to complain, then checked herself and stayed silent.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是宗教上的「占卜」，而是較文學性的用法，表示「敏銳地猜到、看出來」。
generatedExample: From her expression, he divined that she already knew the truth.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「無產階級／底層大眾」，是《一九八四》裡的特定用法，不是一般日常字面意思。
generatedExample: The proles were kept away from the centers of power.

explanationZhTw: 這是小說中的造詞，指「舊語／舊式英語」，也就是被新政權取代前的傳統語言。
generatedExample: In the novel, Oldspeak is slowly replaced by official language.

explanationZhTw: 這也是小說中的造詞，指被政權控制的「新語」，用來限制思考、扭曲原意。
generatedExample: The regime taught citizens to speak in Newspeak.

explanationZhTw: 「口號；標語」。在這裡指政黨用來宣傳理念的短句。
generatedExample: The company changed its slogan to attract younger customers.

explanationZhTw: 「概念；觀念」。這裡指「自由」這個抽象想法。
generatedExample: The concept of justice means different things in different cultures.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是天氣，而是比喻「整體思想氛圍、社會普遍想法的環境」。
generatedExample: The climate of thought in the city changed after the election.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡指「正統觀念；盲目服從官方立場」，不是只指宗教上的正統。
generatedExample: In that system, orthodoxy was more important than independent judgment.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「vaporized」不是字面上的「汽化」，而是指被政權秘密處理、抹除，甚至殺掉。
generatedExample: Several officials were vaporized after they fell out of favor.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是單純「不見」，而是指被政府帶走、抹除痕跡，從社會上消失。
generatedExample: If he keeps asking questions, he may disappear overnight.

explanationZhTw: 這是慣用說法，意思是他的表情已經把想法全透露出來，不需要他明說。
generatedExample: Her fear was written in her face before she opened the door.

explanationZhTw: 聲音尖銳刺耳，帶有強烈、不舒服的感覺。
generatedExample: Her strident voice made everyone in the room look up.

explanationZhTw: 毫不留情地；持續不停地。
generatedExample: The rain fell remorselessly all night.

explanationZhTw: 熱切地；急切地。
generatedExample: The students eagerly opened the new book.

explanationZhTw: 一瞬間；片刻。
generatedExample: For an instant, nobody in the room moved.

explanationZhTw: 只憑外表見過而認得，不代表真的熟識。
generatedExample: I know her by sight, but I have never spoken to her.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 post 指「職位／職務」，不是「郵寄」或「貼文」。
generatedExample: She was offered an important post in the company.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「眼鏡反光」，看起來像兩個空白圓盤。
generatedExample: Her glasses caught the light and flashed briefly.

explanationZhTw: 完整地；沒有被打散或弄壞。
generatedExample: The package arrived in one piece.

explanationZhTw: 擬聲詞，模仿鴨叫聲；這裡用來貶低那人的說話像亂叫。
generatedExample: The pond was quiet except for the quack-quacking of ducks.

explanationZhTw: 公開指責；痛斥。
generatedExample: The politician was denouncing corruption in public.

explanationZhTw: 激烈抨擊；猛烈發言。
generatedExample: He spent the meeting fulminating against the new rules.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「掉進沉默」，而是指「突然不再說話；安靜下來」。
generatedExample: After the question, the whole room had fallen silent.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「quacked」不是單純指鴨叫，而是帶有貶義地形容說話聲尖銳、刺耳，像一直喋喋不休。
generatedExample: He quacked on about politics until everyone got tired.

explanationZhTw: 「audible」表示「聽得見的；可被聽到的」，常用來描述在嘈雜環境中仍能聽清楚。
generatedExample: Her voice was barely audible over the engine noise.

explanationZhTw: 「din」指大聲、令人煩躁的雜音或喧鬧聲，常用於描述吵雜的環境。
generatedExample: We shouted to each other above the din of the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 「din」指大聲、令人煩躁的雜音或喧鬧聲，常用於描述吵雜的環境。
generatedExample: We shouted to each other above the din of the crowd.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》裡的 Newspeak 造詞，字面上是「像鴨子一樣嘎嘎叫」；在小說語境中，它是作者刻意造出的專有語詞，用來呈現極權語言的荒謬與操控性。
generatedExample: In the novel, duckspeak is used to show how language can be twisted for control.

explanationZhTw: 指「互相矛盾的兩種意思」。這裡強調同一個詞可以依語境帶有相反的褒貶色彩。
generatedExample: The word has contradictory meanings depending on the context.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 applied to 不是「施加到」的字面動作，而是「用在／形容某人或某事」的意思。
generatedExample: Applied to a politician, the same phrase can sound like an insult.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡不是字面上的「汽化」，而是黨國用語中「被秘密處決、抹除」的意思。
generatedExample: After the failed mission, the traitor was vaporized.

explanationZhTw: 指「思想犯」，也就是因為想法本身就被政權定罪的人，是這部小說裡的專有政治詞。
generatedExample: In that country, anyone who questioned the leader could be labeled a thought-criminal.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「謹慎、懂得分寸」的意思，表示知道什麼話該說、什麼話不該說。
generatedExample: She handled the matter with great discretion.

explanationZhTw: 指「冷淡、疏離感」，說的是他和別人保持距離的氣質。
generatedExample: His aloofness made the rest of the team uncomfortable.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是一般的「愚蠢」，而是帶點反諷地說「適度的傻氣反而能保命」，因為不會想太多、也不會說太多。
generatedExample: Sometimes a little saving stupidity is useful in a dangerous place.

explanationZhTw: 指「不合正統的、非主流的」。在這裡是說他其實並不算偏離黨的標準。
generatedExample: They took an unorthodox route to solve the problem.

explanationZhTw: 指「崇敬、尊崇」，語氣很強，帶有近乎宗教式的敬拜感。
generatedExample: The old monument was venerated by people from the whole region.

explanationZhTw: 指「經常去、常常光顧」。這裡是在說他很常去那家咖啡館。
generatedExample: They frequented the same bookstore every weekend.

explanationZhTw: 指「不祥的、帶厄運感的」，不是說真的有什麼超自然現象，而是氣氛令人覺得不吉利。
generatedExample: The empty corridor felt ill-omened after midnight.

explanationZhTw: 在政治語境裡指「清洗、剷除」，通常暗示被逮捕、消失或處死。
generatedExample: The regime purged anyone who showed disloyalty.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 orthodoxy 不是宗教上的「正統」，而是指政治上的「正統思想、標準信條」；整句是在說盲目順從就是失去意識。
generatedExample: In that system, orthodoxy mattered more than evidence.

explanationZhTw: 「looked up」在這裡表示「抬起頭／把視線從別處移開看向上方」，是很常見的片語動詞用法。
generatedExample: When I heard my name, I looked up from the book.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是慣用說法，意思是「小心地穿過、迂迴前進」，通常指在人群或障礙物之間穿行。
generatedExample: She threaded her way through the crowded station without stopping.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 revert to 不是一般的「恢復」抽象用法，而是指「又回到某種舊習慣或固定穿法」，也就是又改穿短褲。
generatedExample: After the presentation, he reverted to his usual relaxed tone.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，意思是「散發出、發出」，這裡指身上散出濃重的汗味。
generatedExample: The machine was giving off a loud buzzing noise.

explanationZhTw: 字面是「一顆顆水珠」，這裡是帶有形象感的說法，實際上指臉上冒出的汗珠。
generatedExample: Beads of moisture formed on the glass after he poured in the cold drink.

explanationZhTw: 表示持續不停地做事，帶有「埋頭苦幹、一直忙著做」的語氣。
generatedExample: She was working away on her essay until midnight.

explanationZhTw: 英式口語，常用來稱呼熟識的男性，帶有親暱或半客套的語氣，類似「老兄、老弟」。
generatedExample: Come on, old boy, have a seat and relax.

explanationZhTw: 口語形容人很聰明、很會讀書或腦筋很好。
generatedExample: My sister is the brainy one in our family.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是字面上的「追逐」，而是口語表示「一直在找你、追著你要東西」。
generatedExample: I've been chasing you all afternoon to sign this form.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是縮略語，要靠上下文理解，通常指「subscription」之類的訂閱費或會費。
generatedExample: I forgot to pay my sub this month.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `sub` 是 `subscription` 的縮寫，意思是「訂閱費、會費、定期繳的款項」。
generatedExample: I forgot to pay my magazine sub this month.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `automatically` 不是「自動地」的機械意思，而是指「不自覺地、下意識地」。
generatedExample: She automatically reached for her phone when it rang.

explanationZhTw: `earmarked` 在這裡表示「預先撥出、指定用途」，不是字面上的「標記耳朵」。
generatedExample: We earmarked part of the budget for repairs.

explanationZhTw: `voluntary subscriptions` 指「自願性的訂閱費／捐款」，在這裡是指必須自己付的各種固定款項。
generatedExample: The club relies on voluntary subscriptions from members.

explanationZhTw: `keep track of` 是片語動詞，意思是「持續掌握、追蹤、記住」某些東西的狀態或數量。
generatedExample: I use a notebook to keep track of my expenses.

explanationZhTw: 「Hate Week」是這部小說裡特定的政治宣傳活動名稱，不是一般字面上的「憎恨的一週」。
generatedExample: Everyone in the office was busy preparing for Hate Week.

explanationZhTw: 「house-by-house fund」指挨家挨戶募款或收錢的活動，屬於語境特定的說法。
generatedExample: They organized a house-by-house fund to pay for the repairs.

explanationZhTw: 「treasurer」是負責保管或管理金錢的人，也就是財務/出納負責人。
generatedExample: She is the treasurer for the club this year.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「block」不是單純的「方塊」，而是指街區、同一區域的一排建築。
generatedExample: We live on the same block as the library.

explanationZhTw: 「all-out effort」表示全力以赴、盡最大努力。
generatedExample: The team made an all-out effort to finish before the deadline.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「put on」不是「穿上」，而是「上演、舉辦、演出」。
generatedExample: The school will put on a play next month.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「outfit」不是「服裝」，而是指一整組、整套的東西；這裡就是「一整組旗子」。
generatedExample: They bought a colorful outfit of decorations for the party.

explanationZhTw: 「handed over」是片語動詞，表示把某物交給對方、移交。
generatedExample: She handed over the package at the reception desk.

explanationZhTw: 「filthy」表示非常髒的；這裡用來形容紙條又皺又髒。
generatedExample: His boots were filthy after walking through the mud.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「entered」不是單純「進入」，而是指把內容寫入、記錄到筆記本裡。
generatedExample: The assistant entered the details into the system.

explanationZhTw: 「illiterate」指不識字的、沒受過讀寫能力訓練的人。
generatedExample: He was illiterate when he arrived in the city.

explanationZhTw: 口語用語，表示「順帶一提、對了」。這裡不是字面上的「經過這條路」。
generatedExample: By the way, did you finish the report?

explanationZhTw: 英式口語的稱呼語，對男性說話時帶點親暱或寒暄的味道，並不一定真的指「老」。
generatedExample: Good to see you, old boy.

explanationZhTw: 帶點戲謔或半寵溺的說法，字面是「小乞丐」，但這裡只是稱那個孩子有點淘氣。
generatedExample: That little beggar hid my keys again.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「突然發射、猛然攻擊」；此處指用彈弓朝某人射擊。
generatedExample: He let fly with a stone at the fence.

explanationZhTw: 名詞用法，指「嚴厲訓斥、痛罵一頓」。
generatedExample: She got a dressing-down for being late.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞 `take away`，這裡是「把某物拿走、沒收」的意思。
generatedExample: The teacher said he would take the phone away.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `upset` 表示「不高興、難過、心情受影響」，是很常見的口語用法；在這句話中，它描述的是因為不能去看處決而感到失望或不悅。
generatedExample: She was upset when her friend canceled the meeting.

explanationZhTw: `execution` 在這裡不是「執行工作」的普通意思，而是指「處決」，也就是依法或官方決定而殺死某人。這是比較正式、帶有特定語境的用法。
generatedExample: The prisoners were taken away before the execution.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `right spirit` 不是字面上的「正確的精神」，而是指「態度對、很合乎期待、很有那種勁頭」；屬於情境化用法。
generatedExample: She answered the complaint in the right spirit and stayed calm.

explanationZhTw: `keenness` 在這裡是「熱衷、熱心、積極」的意思，不是「鋒利」；屬於較抽象的詞義。
generatedExample: I was impressed by her keenness to learn new skills.

explanationZhTw: `slipped off` 是片語動詞，意思是「悄悄離開、溜走」，帶有不聲不響地走開的感覺。
generatedExample: He slipped off before the meeting ended.

explanationZhTw: `kept on his tail` 是慣用語，意思是「一路緊跟著、盯著不放」。
generatedExample: The reporter kept on the minister's tail until he answered the question.

explanationZhTw: `handed him over` 是片語動詞，意思是「把某人交給別人處理」或「移交給另一方」。
generatedExample: The guards handed him over to the police.

explanationZhTw: 「taken aback」表示「被嚇一跳、吃驚」，用來形容 Winston 對前一句話感到意外而一時愣住。
generatedExample: I was taken aback by how quickly the meeting changed direction.

explanationZhTw: 「went on」在這裡是「接著說、繼續說下去」的意思，不是字面上的「走上去」。
generatedExample: She paused for a moment, then went on explaining the plan.

explanationZhTw: `make sure` 在這裡是「確定、保證」的意思，表示他把這件事弄得很肯定。
generatedExample: I made sure the door was locked before we left.

explanationZhTw: `put someone on to someone` 在這裡是「讓某人注意到、懷疑到某人」；不是字面上的「放上去」。
generatedExample: The clue put the police on to the real suspect.

explanationZhTw: `funny` 這裡不是「好笑」，而是「奇怪的、怪異的」意思。
generatedExample: That is a funny-looking hat; I have never seen one like it.

explanationZhTw: `nipper` 是英式口語，指「小孩、孩子」，帶點親暱或隨口的語氣。
generatedExample: When I was a nipper, I used to spend all day outside.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `What happened to ...?` 是在問「……後來怎麼了／發生了什麼事」，常用來追問某人的去向或結果，不是單純詢問事件本身。
generatedExample: What happened to the dog after the storm?

explanationZhTw: 「說不上來／我也不確定」；這裡是委婉地表示自己無法回答。
generatedExample: I couldn't say for sure whether he'll come.

explanationZhTw: 「完全驚訝」在這裡是反向說法，整句意思接近「我一點也不會太意外」。
generatedExample: I wouldn't be altogether surprised if the meeting runs late.

explanationZhTw: 發出咂嘴聲；這裡是用舌頭快速發聲，模擬爆炸聲。
generatedExample: She clicked her tongue in disapproval.

explanationZhTw: `abstractedly` 表示「心不在焉地、若有所思地」。這是較文學或書面語的用法，描述人說話時注意力不在眼前事物上。
generatedExample: He nodded abstractedly while reading the report.

explanationZhTw: `look up` 在這裡不是「查詢」，而是指「抬頭、把視線從手上的東西移開」。句中的意思是席梅一直盯著紙條看，沒有抬頭。
generatedExample: She answered without looking up from her notebook.

explanationZhTw: 「afford to do」在這裡不是「買得起」，而是指「承擔得起、冒得起」；整句表示「我們當然不能冒險」。
generatedExample: We can't afford to make mistakes in this project.

explanationZhTw: 慣用語，意思是「冒險、碰運氣」；在這裡指故意去做不穩妥的事。
generatedExample: Don't take chances when the road is icy.

explanationZhTw: 這個副詞表示「恭順地、順從地、照規矩地」；這裡帶出 Winston 說話時很順服的態度。
generatedExample: She dutifully completed every task on the list.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `there is a war on` 不是字面上的「有一場戰爭在上面」，而是指「現在正處於戰爭狀態／正在打仗」。
generatedExample: There is a war on, so travel has become dangerous.

explanationZhTw: 「As though」表示「好像、彷彿」，用來引出一種帶有推測或比喻感的描述。
generatedExample: As though she were late, he hurried to open the door.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「floated」不是單純指「漂浮」，而是形容聲音「輕飄飄地傳來、飄過來」，屬於文學式的用法。
generatedExample: A soft melody floated through the open window.

explanationZhTw: 「telescreen」是小說中的特殊裝置名稱，指帶有監視與廣播功能的螢幕。
generatedExample: The telescreen stayed on all night.

explanationZhTw: 「However」表示「然而、但是」，用來轉折前後語意。
generatedExample: I wanted to go out; however, it started raining.

explanationZhTw: 「proclamation」是較正式的「公告、宣告」，通常指公開而鄭重的聲明。
generatedExample: The king made a proclamation to the whole city.

explanationZhTw: 「merely」表示「只不過、僅僅」，用來降低事件的重要性。
generatedExample: It was merely a small mistake.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `Comrades` 是「同志、同伴」的意思，帶有群體認同和政治宣傳色彩。
generatedExample: The teacher greeted the class with, 'Good morning, comrades.'

explanationZhTw: `eager` 表示「熱切的、急切想要做某事的」，在這裡形容說話聲音很熱情、迫不及待。
generatedExample: She was eager to start her new job.

explanationZhTw: 這是比喻說法，把生產目標說成一場戰役；在這裡意思是「生產方面已經取得勝利／達成成果」。
generatedExample: The company says it has won the battle for customer trust.

explanationZhTw: `output` 在這裡不是一般的「輸出」，而是指「產量、產出量」。
generatedExample: Factory output increased after the new machines arrived.

explanationZhTw: `consumption goods` 指「消費品」，也就是供人使用、購買和消耗的商品。
generatedExample: The report compares prices for basic consumption goods.

explanationZhTw: `standard of living` 是「生活水準、生活水平」，指一個地區或人群的平均生活品質。
generatedExample: The average standard of living improved over the decade.

explanationZhTw: `demonstrations` 在這裡不是「抗議示威」，而是指大規模公開表態、遊行或慶祝活動；語境是被官方正面包裝的群眾活動。
generatedExample: There were street demonstrations after the team won the championship.

explanationZhTw: `marched out` 表示「列隊走出、整齊地走出來」；這裡帶有群體一致行動的感覺。
generatedExample: The students marched out of the hall after the assembly ended.

explanationZhTw: `bestowed upon us` 是很正式、帶點古典的說法，意思是「賜予我們、給予我們」。
generatedExample: The award was bestowed upon her for her research.

explanationZhTw: `Foodstuffs` 是較正式的「食品、食物」總稱，常指各種食材或可食用商品。
generatedExample: The warehouse stores imported foodstuffs and drinks.

explanationZhTw: 表示「反覆出現、再次發生」。這裡是說這個短語一再被提到。
generatedExample: The same warning recurred throughout the report.

explanationZhTw: 表示「近來、最近」，是較文雅的說法。
generatedExample: Of late, she has been arriving earlier than usual.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是帶諷刺的說法，指「張口發愣又故作莊重」的樣子。
generatedExample: He listened in gaping solemnity, as if the speech were profound.

explanationZhTw: 表示「理解那些數字或計算內容」。
generatedExample: I could not follow the figures in the budget discussion.

explanationZhTw: 表示「費力地拖出、搬出」，常帶有笨重、不輕鬆的感覺。
generatedExample: She lugged out an old suitcase from the attic.

explanationZhTw: 表示「對某事充耳不聞、刻意忽略」。
generatedExample: He shut his ears to the complaints and kept working.

explanationZhTw: 表示「源源不絕地流出、湧出」，這裡指聲音從螢幕中持續傳出。
generatedExample: Music streamed out of the open windows all evening.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「抗議示威」，而是帶諷刺意味的「公開表態／集體表示支持」。
generatedExample: The government claimed there were demonstrations of support in the square.

explanationZhTw: 表示「竟然接受那種說法」，含有「毫不懷疑地吞下」的意思。
generatedExample: I cannot believe he swallowed that excuse.

explanationZhTw: 表示「追查到、找出來」，常指把人或事物找出來。
generatedExample: Police are trying to track down the source of the leak.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡是誇張且可怕的用法，指「徹底消滅、殺掉」，不是字面上的「蒸發」。
generatedExample: The dictator threatened to vaporize his enemies.

explanationZhTw: 這是《1984》中的專有概念，指同時接受互相矛盾的兩種想法。
generatedExample: The novel uses doublethink to show how propaganda distorts logic.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 fabulous 不是單純「極好」，而是帶有誇張、宣傳甚至諷刺的語氣，暗示這些數據可疑地漂亮。
generatedExample: The ad was full of fabulous statistics that sounded too good to be true.

explanationZhTw: 源源不絕地湧出；這裡指數據從電視螢幕大量播報出來。
generatedExample: Reports poured out of the office all morning.

explanationZhTw: 拿起並開始做某事；這裡是拿起湯匙。
generatedExample: She took up tennis after moving to Taipei.

explanationZhTw: 輕輕沾弄、隨手攪動；在這裡是指用湯匙在肉汁裡畫來畫去。
generatedExample: He was dabbling in the soup with a spoon to cool it.

explanationZhTw: 佈滿髒污的、灰黑骯髒的，常用來形容長期沒清理的表面。
generatedExample: The kitchen walls were grimy after years of smoke.

explanationZhTw: 搖搖晃晃、不穩固的；這裡形容家具破舊又不牢靠。
generatedExample: The old wooden bridge was rickety and unsafe.

explanationZhTw: 味道極差、令人作嘔；這裡是形容咖啡非常難喝。
generatedExample: The milk had a filthy-tasting aftertaste.

explanationZhTw: 漫長得像沒有盡頭，讓人很不耐煩或很難受。
generatedExample: The meeting was interminable, so we left early.

explanationZhTw: 散掉、裂開成碎片；這裡指香菸一碰就碎。
generatedExample: The cardboard box came to pieces in the rain.

explanationZhTw: 祖先記憶；在這裡指一種模糊、近乎本能地覺得「以前應該不是這樣」的深層感受。
generatedExample: She had a vague ancestral memory of living near the sea.

explanationZhTw: looked round = 四下張望、環顧四周。
generatedExample: She looked round the room before answering.

explanationZhTw: canteen 指「餐廳、員工餐廳」；這裡是大家一起吃飯的地方。
generatedExample: I had lunch in the canteen with my coworkers.

explanationZhTw: look about you 在這裡是「留意周遭、看清周圍情況」。
generatedExample: Look about you before you cross the road.

explanationZhTw: set up by 在這裡不是「放上去」，而是「被建立、被塑造成」。
generatedExample: The school set up a new system for late arrivals.

explanationZhTw: predominated 表示「占多數、最常見」；這裡是說這種外型看起來好像成了主流。
generatedExample: Red jackets predominated in the crowd.

explanationZhTw: ill-favoured 是較文學、舊式的說法，指「長相不佳、不好看」。
generatedExample: The painting showed an ill-favoured man with a hard stare.

explanationZhTw: proliferated 在這裡是「大量增多、迅速擴散」，不是字面上的繁殖。
generatedExample: False rumors proliferated online after the incident.

explanationZhTw: 「announcement」是「公告、宣告」；這裡指官方對外公布的訊息。
generatedExample: The announcement about the new schedule surprised everyone.

explanationZhTw: 「gave way to」表示「轉而變成、接著變為、讓位給」；這裡是說公告結束後，接著換成刺耳的音樂。
generatedExample: The rain gave way to bright sunshine in the afternoon.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的「stirred」不是字面上的「攪動」，而是「激起、引發」情緒反應。
generatedExample: The speech stirred a sense of hope in the audience.

explanationZhTw: 「bombardment」原意是「轟炸」；這裡是比喻用法，表示大量數字接連出現，讓人有被資訊狂轟濫炸的感覺。
generatedExample: He sat through a bombardment of figures during the budget meeting.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `done a good job` 表示「做得很好、表現不錯」，是很常見的口語評價，用來稱讚某件事完成得有效率或有成果。
generatedExample: They did a good job organizing the event.

explanationZhTw: `knowing` 在這裡不是單純「知道」，而是帶有「心照不宣、表示他明白內情」的語氣，整個片語描述一種有暗示意味的動作。
generatedExample: She smiled with a knowing look.

explanationZhTw: `By the way` 是轉話題的常用說法，意思是「順帶一提、對了」。
generatedExample: By the way, did you finish the report?

explanationZhTw: `old boy` 是英式口語中的稱呼，語氣很隨便，常像中文的「老兄、老弟」；這裡不是字面上的「老男孩」。
generatedExample: Come on, old boy, help me move this box.

explanationZhTw: `I suppose` 表示「我想、我猜、我認為」，語氣比直接說 `I think` 更委婉。
generatedExample: I suppose we should leave now.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 `blade` 不是一般「刀刃」的抽象意思，而是指「剃刀刀片／刮鬍刀片」。在這句話裡，它是日常物品名稱，但語境很重要，因為前文在討論刮鬍或修整用具。
generatedExample: I need a new blade because this one is too dull to shave with.

explanationZhTw: 英式口語中對男性的稱呼，帶點熟稔或客氣，意思接近「老兄／老弟／老朋友」，不是字面上的「老男孩」。
generatedExample: Thanks for the help, old boy.

explanationZhTw: 「sorry」是常見的 A2 級詞彙，意思是「抱歉；對不起」。這裡是 Winston 直接說出的道歉語氣。
generatedExample: Sorry, I didn't mean to be late.

explanationZhTw: 「announcement」是「公告、宣佈」，指正式對外傳達的訊息。
generatedExample: We heard an announcement about the train delay.

explanationZhTw: 「started up」在這裡表示「又開始發出聲音／重新響起」，不是單純的「啟動」。
generatedExample: The old radio started up again after I touched the dial.

explanationZhTw: 「wispy」形容頭髮細軟、稀疏、輕飄飄的樣子。
generatedExample: She had wispy hair that moved in the wind.

explanationZhTw: 「creases」是「皺摺、摺痕」，這裡指臉上的紋路裡積了灰塵。
generatedExample: There were deep creases in his shirt after the trip.

explanationZhTw: 「denouncing」在這裡指「檢舉、告發、公開指控」某人。
generatedExample: He denounced the plan as unfair and dangerous.

explanationZhTw: 「Thought Police」是《1984》裡的專有名詞，指監控思想與異議的秘密警察。
generatedExample: The novel's Thought Police made private thoughts a crime.

explanationZhTw: 「on the other hand」是慣用語，表示「另一方面、相對地」，用來對比前面的內容。
generatedExample: I like the design; on the other hand, the price is too high.

explanationZhTw: 在這部小說裡，「vaporized」不是字面上的「汽化」，而是指被政權處死、抹除存在。
generatedExample: Anyone accused by the regime could be vaporized overnight.

explanationZhTw: 「labyrinthine」形容像迷宮一樣複雜、曲折；這裡強調走廊系統很繁複。
generatedExample: We got lost in the labyrinthine corridors of the old hospital.

explanationZhTw: 「instinctively」指「憑直覺地、出於本能地」，不是經過理性思考。
generatedExample: She instinctively stepped back when the dog barked.

explanationZhTw: 「perish」是較正式的「死亡、滅亡」，比一般的 die 更書面。
generatedExample: Without water, the plants will perish quickly.

explanationZhTw: 「survival」是「生存、存活」，也可指「倖存下來的狀態」。
generatedExample: Their survival depended on finding shelter before nightfall.

explanationZhTw: 指把人從出神、沉思或白日夢般的狀態猛然打斷。
generatedExample: The loud notification dragged me out of my reverie.

explanationZhTw: 指側著、斜斜地看，通常帶有從側面偷看或不直接正視的感覺。
generatedExample: He gave her a sidelong look before answering.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示她一和他對上眼，立刻被他注意到或和他四目相接。
generatedExample: The bright poster caught his eye as soon as he entered the station.

explanationZhTw: 這裡不是「出發」，而是指「開始冒出、顯現出來」。
generatedExample: A cold sweat started out on his forehead before the interview.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 pang 指「突如其來的一陣強烈感受」，常用來形容痛楚、後悔或恐懼。
generatedExample: She felt a pang of regret after sending the message.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示恐懼突然湧上全身，讓人強烈地感受到。
generatedExample: A wave of panic went through him when the lights went out.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 nagging 不是「嘮叨的」，而是指「揮之不去、一直讓人不舒服的」。
generatedExample: He had a nagging feeling that he had missed an important detail.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是英式用法，表示「跟著某人四處走、一路尾隨」。
generatedExample: The dog kept following him about the house.

explanationZhTw: 這是轉折用語，表示「不管怎樣／至少」。
generatedExample: At any rate, we should finish the report today.

explanationZhTw: apparent 這裡表示「表面上看來的」，整體是說「看不出有必要」。
generatedExample: There was no apparent need to change the plan.

explanationZhTw: 這裡的 object 不是「物品」，而是指「目的、意圖」。
generatedExample: The real object of the meeting was to settle the argument.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「確認、確保」。
generatedExample: Please make sure the door is locked before you leave.

explanationZhTw: 「思想警察」；在這裡指監視並懲罰人們思想與言行的秘密警察，不是一般的警察單位。
generatedExample: He feared the Thought Police would notice even a small mistake.

explanationZhTw: 「在電幕可監視的範圍內」；這裡的 range 指被監控或可接收訊號的範圍。
generatedExample: Keep your voice low when you are within range of the security camera.

explanationZhTw: 「暴露你、洩露你的祕密」；指讓別人看出你有問題、在隱瞞事情或不自然。
generatedExample: A nervous smile could give you away.

explanationZhTw: 「喃喃自語」；在這裡是一種會被認為可疑的舉動。
generatedExample: He was muttering to himself while reading the note.

explanationZhTw: 「臉罪」；這是 Newspeak 裡捏造的詞，指臉上出現不被允許的表情本身就是罪行。
generatedExample: In that society, a bad expression could be treated as FACECRIME.

explanationZhTw: 把背轉向某人；這裡指故意不理會、迴避或冷落他。
generatedExample: When he tried to apologize, she turned her back on him.

explanationZhTw: 畢竟；用來修正前面的猜測，表示重新考慮後的判斷。
generatedExample: I thought the store was closed, but it was open after all.

explanationZhTw: 跟著他到處走、一路尾隨；這裡指持續監視或跟蹤他的行動。
generatedExample: He felt uneasy, as if someone were following him about.

explanationZhTw: 連續兩天；這裡表示事情在兩天內連續發生。
generatedExample: It rained two days running, so the match was postponed.

explanationZhTw: （火、燈、香菸等）熄掉了；這裡指香菸不再燃燒。
generatedExample: My candle went out when the window opened.

explanationZhTw: 把某物收好、藏起來；這裡指把紙條收進口袋。
generatedExample: She stowed the documents away in a drawer.

explanationZhTw: 英式口語稱呼，類似「老兄／老弟」，帶熟稔或戲謔語氣。
generatedExample: Thanks for the help, old boy.

explanationZhTw: 口語中指「小孩、小鬼頭」；有點俏皮或親暱。
generatedExample: The nippers are playing in the garden.

explanationZhTw: 使……著火；縱火。
generatedExample: Someone set fire to the old shed last night.

explanationZhTw: 悄悄地從後面靠近。
generatedExample: He sneaked up behind me and shouted.

explanationZhTw: 口語裡半抱怨半親暱地稱小鬼頭、小傢伙。
generatedExample: Those little beggars stole all the cookies.

explanationZhTw: 非常熱衷、很起勁。
generatedExample: She was keen as mustard to join the team.

explanationZhTw: 這裡指「一流的、很棒的」，不是字面上的「第一等」。
generatedExample: They did a first-rate job on the repairs.

explanationZhTw: 這裡表示「配發／發給他們」。
generatedExample: The company served the staff out with new laptops.

explanationZhTw: 老式喇叭形聽筒或助聽器。
generatedExample: An ear trumpet was used before modern hearing aids.

explanationZhTw: 試用、試驗一下。
generatedExample: I tried it out before I bought it.

explanationZhTw: 這裡是「認為、覺得」；不是「計算」。
generatedExample: I reckoned he would arrive late.

explanationZhTw: 《1984》中的特定名詞，指兼具螢幕與監視功能的裝置，不是一般日常英文常用詞。
generatedExample: The telescreen in the room flashed a warning message.

explanationZhTw: 在這裡作名詞，表示「訊號、暗號」，屬於基礎但常見的實用詞。
generatedExample: The bell was the signal to start the meeting.

explanationZhTw: 表示「回去工作／恢復工作」，是常見的動作表達。
generatedExample: After lunch, we returned to work right away.

explanationZhTw: 慣用說法，表示突然站起來，帶有迅速、反射式的動作感。
generatedExample: When the door opened, everyone sprang to their feet.

explanationZhTw: 片語動詞，表示「加入、參與某個活動或行動」。
generatedExample: You can join in if you want to play.
